\boxed{C}
Based on the detailed evidence gathered, the pulp proper indeed includes both T and B lymphocytes, aligning closely with prior conclusions drawn. Specifically, the white pulp portion of the spleen, analogous to parts of the pulp proper in teeth, prominently features both B-cells residing in germinal centers and T-cells scattered elsewhere like the marginal zone. Thus, considering the diversity and coexistence of these lymphocyte types within similar niches described here for dental anatomy, my agreement stands firmly behind recognizing multiple lymphocyte varieties inhabiting the pulp proper environment collectively. Therefore, I reaffirm my initial stance favoring Option C - both T and B lymphocytes predominate in this setting.
